converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
George H. Sabine papers
Sabine, George Holland, 1880-1961.
14-21-547
5 cubic feet.
1886-1960 Notes, drafts, correspondence, scrapbook. Largely notes for and rough drafts of the revised edition of Sabine's chief work, A History of Political Theory, as well as additional books and correspondence. Collection material in English 2093131
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
JK
Date completed:
December 12, 1961
EAD encoding:
Ann Hubert, October 2003
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Professor of Philosophy, dean of the Graduate School, and vice president of Cornell University.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Largely notes for and rough drafts of the revised edition of Sabine's chief work, A History of Political Theory; supplemented by four books with underlining and marginal comments: A Treastise of Human Nature; After Utopia; Critique of Pure Reason; and What Is to Be Done. Includes reprints, book reviews, and examinations and other material pertaining to his professional life; a scrapbook (1900-1905) on his student days at Cornell; notes and correspondence concerning the publication of the diary of Cornell's first president, Andrew Dickson White; and genealogical papers on the Sabine family. Administrative and professional correspondence, and drafts and copies of Sabine's book reviews. Includes correspondence concerning Sabine's participation in the American Political Science Association. Correspondents include George Boas, Arthur C. Cole, Arthur H. Cole, Gustavus Watts Cunningham, Edmund Ezra Day, Dixon Ryan Fox, Ludwig F. Freund, George H. Gallup, Milton R. Konvitz, Harold J. Laski, Glenn R. Morrow, Frank Thilly, Charles M. Wiltse, and Carl F. Wittke.
Cite As:
George H. Sabine papers, [ca. 1886-1960] #14-21-547. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Philosophy, Modern. Boas, George, 1891- Cole, Arthur Charles, 1886-1976. Cole, Arthur Harrison, 1889- Cunningham, Gustavus Watts, 1881-1968 Day, Edmund Ezra, 1883-1951. Fox, Dixon Ryan, 1887-1945. Freund, Ludwig Franz, 1898- Gallup, George, 1901-1984. Konvitz, Milton R. (Milton Ridvas), 1908-2003. Laski, Harold J. (Harold Joseph), 1893-1950. Morrow, Glenn R. (Glenn Raymond), 1895- Thilly, Frank, 1865-1934. Wiltse, Charles M. (Charles Maurice), 1907-1990. Wittke, Carl Frederick, 1892-1971. Cornell University. Department of Philosophy Cornell University. Graduate School Cornell University -- : Faculty. Cornell University -- : Administration. American Political Science Association

Morse regarding William C. Pruyn 1903 , check forger19031School and Society,Sept. 12, 19311Lenin, V.I., What Is To Be Done?1Packet of cards relating to membership in the "Royal Society"1Kant, Critique of Pure Reason1Shklar, After Utopia1Hume, Treastise of Human Nature1Family Genealogy within volume entitled "Record"1International Journal of Ethics, Jan. 1926, Apr. 1927 (two issues)Jan. 1926Apr. 19271Yale Law Journal,Dec. 19401Ethics,July 19381Journal of Philosopy, Psychology and Scientific Methods,Nov. 25, 19151Journal of Philosophy,June 4, 19311Sabine, George H.Feb. 1939Nov. 1905Sept. 1912July 1920Jan. 1906Nov. 1923July 1912Jan. 1917Mar. 1907Nov. 1930Aug. 1928May 1907Jan. 19241
Scope and Contents
"What is Political Theory?" reprint from the Journal of Politics, Feb. 1939
"Radical Empiricism as a Logical Method" reprint from Philosophical Review, Nov. 1905
"Professor Bosanquet's Logic and the Concrete Universal" reprint from PR, Sept. 1912
"The Concept of the State as Power" reprint from PR, July 1920
"Hume's Contribution to the Historical Method" reprint from PR, Jan. 1906
"Bosanquet's Theory of the Real Will" reprint from PR, Nov. 1923 (2 copies)
"Descriptive and Normative Sciences" reprint from PR, July 1912
"Philosophical and Scientific Specialization" reprint from PR, Jan. 1917
"The Concreteness of Thought" reprint from PR, Mar. 1907
"The Pragmatic Approach to Political Science" reprint from American Political Science Review, Nov. 1930
"Political Science and the Juristic Point of View" reprint from APSR, Aug. 1928
"The Material of Thought" reprint from PR, May 1907
"Henry Adams and the Writing of History" reprint from the University of California Chronical, Jan. 1924Vlastos, Gregory, "Equality and Justice in Early Greek Cosmologies" reprint from Classical Philology,July 19471Review of Nesbit's The Quest for Community reprinted from the Southwestern Social Science Quarterly,June 19531Sabine, G.H.May 1945Mar. 1939Jan. 19481941Sept. 19471
Scope and Contents
"Democracy and Preconceived Ideas" Lecture delivered at Ohio State, May 1945
"Logic and Social Studies" reprint from Philosophical Review, Mar. 1939
Review of Irving Babbit Book Democracy and Leadership reprinted from University of California Chronical, Apr. 1925
"Beyond Ideology" reprint from Philosophical Review, Jan. 1948
"Social Studies and Objectivity" reprint from University of California Publications in Philosophy, 1941
"Freedom and Reform" Philosophical Review, Sept. 1947
